# R package **estimability**: Support for determining estimability of linear functions

[![cran
version](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/estimability)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=estimability)
[![downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/estimability)](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/estimability)
[![total
downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/estimability)](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/estimability)

## Website:

<https://rvlenth.github.io/estimability/>

## Overview

When a model is rank-deficient, predictions from that model may not be
unique. The `estimability` R package provides a set of tools to assess
whether predictions from a rank-deficient model are unique (i.e., are
estimable). These tools apply to a rich class of models that includes
linear models, generalized linear models, mixed models, and models with
more general correlated-error structures (e.g., spatial models, time
series models). For further details, see
[doi:10.32614/RJ-2015-016](https://journal.r-project.org/articles/RJ-2015-016/).

## Features

- A [`nonest.basis()`](reference/nonest.basis.md) function is provided
  that determines a basis for the null space of a matrix. This may be
  used in conjunction with [`is.estble()`](reference/nonest.basis.md) to
  determine the estimability (within a tolerance) of a given linear
  function of the regression coefficients in a linear model.
- A set of [`epredict()`](reference/epredict.md) methods are provided
  for `lm`, `glm`, and `mlm` objects. These work just like
  [`predict()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/predict.html), except an `NA` is
  returned for any cases that are not estimable. This is a useful
  alternative to the generic warning that “predictions from
  rank-deficient models are unreliable.”
- A function [`estble.subspace()`](reference/estble.subspace.md) that
  projects a set of linear functions onto an  
  estimable subspace (possibly of smaller dimension). This can be useful
  in creating a set of estimable contrasts for joint testing.
- Package developers may wish to import this package and incorporate
  estimability checks for their `predict` methods.

## Installation

- To install latest version from CRAN, run

``` r

install.packages("estimability")
```

Release notes for the latest CRAN version are found at
<https://cran.r-project.org/package=estimability/NEWS> – or do
`news(package = "estimability")` for notes on the version you have
installed.

- To install the latest development version from GitHub, have the newest
  **remotes** package installed, then run

``` r

remotes::install_github("rvlenth/estimability", dependencies = TRUE)
```

For latest release notes on this GitHub version, see the [NEWS
file](https://rvlenth.github.io/estimability/news/index.html)

## Example

Suppose we have four predictors `x1`, `x2`, `x3`, and `x4`, and a
response variable `y`:

``` r

x1 <- -4:4
x2 <- c(-2, 1, -1, 2, 0, 2, -1, 1,-2)
x3 <- 3 * x1 - 2 * x2
x4 <- x2 - x1 + 4
y <- 1 + x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + c(-.5, .5, .5, -.5, 0, .5, -.5, -.5, .5)
dat <- data.frame(x1, x2, x3, x4, y)
head(dat)
#>   x1 x2  x3 x4    y
#> 1 -4 -2  -8  6 -7.5
#> 2 -3  1 -11  8 -3.5
#> 3 -2 -1  -4  5 -0.5
#> 4 -1  2  -7  7  1.5
#> 5  0  0   0  4  5.0
#> 6  1  2  -1  5  8.5
```

If we fit the following two models, different slope coefficients are
estimated as `NA` depending on the order they are provided to the model
formula:

``` r

mod1234 <- lm(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x4, data = dat)
mod4321 <- lm(y ~ x4 + x3 + x2 + x1, data = dat)
zapsmall(rbind(
    b1234 = coef(mod1234),
    b4321 = coef(mod4321)[c(1, 5:2)]
))
#>       (Intercept) x1 x2 x3 x4
#> b1234           5  3  0 NA NA
#> b4321         -19 NA NA  3  6
```

Now suppose our goal is to make predictions of `y` for the new
observations in `testset`:

``` r

testset <- data.frame( 
    x1 = c(3,  6,  6,  0,  0,  1), 
    x2 = c(1,  2,  2,  0,  0,  2), 
    x3 = c(7, 14, 14,  0,  0,  3), 
    x4 = c(2,  4,  0,  4,  0,  4)
)
testset
#>   x1 x2 x3 x4
#> 1  3  1  7  2
#> 2  6  2 14  4
#> 3  6  2 14  0
#> 4  0  0  0  4
#> 5  0  0  0  0
#> 6  1  2  3  4

cbind(
    testset, 
    pred1234 = predict(mod1234, newdata = testset),
    pred4321 = predict(mod4321, newdata = testset)
)
#>   x1 x2 x3 x4 pred1234 pred4321
#> 1  3  1  7  2       14       14
#> 2  6  2 14  4       23       47
#> 3  6  2 14  0       23       23
#> 4  0  0  0  4        5        5
#> 5  0  0  0  0        5      -19
#> 6  1  2  3  4        8       14
```

Predictions for the first, third, and fourth row are the same for both
models, but predictions for the second, fifth, and sixth row are
different. This is a problem, as the underlying data provided to the
models are the same! Note that R does provide a warning about
predictions from the rank-deficient fit (the warning itself is omitted
here).

The `estimability` package remedies these inconsistencies via
[`epredict()`](reference/epredict.md), which indicates the rows of
`testdata` that are estimable (with their predictions) or not estimable
(with `NA`):

``` r

library(estimability)
cbind(
    testset, 
    pred1234 = epredict(mod1234, newdata = testset),
    pred4321 = epredict(mod4321, newdata = testset)
)
#>   x1 x2 x3 x4 pred1234 pred4321
#> 1  3  1  7  2       14       14
#> 2  6  2 14  4       NA       NA
#> 3  6  2 14  0       23       23
#> 4  0  0  0  4        5        5
#> 5  0  0  0  0       NA       NA
#> 6  1  2  3  4       NA       NA
```

Other [`predict()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/predict.html) arguments can
be passed to [`epredict()`](reference/epredict.md), e.g.,:

``` r

epredict(mod1234, newdata = testset, interval = "prediction", level = 0.9)
#>   fit       lwr       upr
#> 1  14 12.715387 15.284613
#> 2  NA        NA        NA
#> 3  23 21.449058 24.550942
#> 4   5  3.817418  6.182582
#> 5  NA        NA        NA
#> 6  NA        NA        NA
```

# Package index

## Overview

- [`estimability-package`](estimability-package.md)
  [`estimability`](estimability-package.md) : Estimability Tools for
  Linear Models

## Tools

- [`nonest.basis()`](nonest.basis.md)
  [`legacy.nonest.basis()`](nonest.basis.md)
  [`is.estble()`](nonest.basis.md) [`all.estble`](nonest.basis.md) :
  Estimability Tools

- [`estble.subspace()`](estble.subspace.md) : Find an estimable subspace

- [`epredict()`](epredict.md) [`eupdate()`](epredict.md) :

  Estimability Enhancements for `lm` and Relatives
